forecast-period.text: A low pressure system over Bass Strait will move eastwards and weaken overnight. An associated cold front will move slowly across the southeast of SA and will clear to the east during Saturday morning. A high pressure system will become established over the Bight during Saturday then will move only slowly eastwards during Sunday and Monday. A low pressure trough will move across southern coastal districts on Tuesday then another high will move across waters to the south.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Southwesterly 20 to 25 knots turning southerly in the early afternoon.
1.5 to 2 metres.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots turning southeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the late morning and afternoon.
1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre around midday.
Southerly 3 to 4 metres, tending southwesterly 2 to 3 metres around midday.
Below 0.5 metres, tending southerly 1 to 2 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Southeasterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to below 10 knots during the morning then becoming east to northeasterly during the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southerly 1 to 2 metres, tending southeasterly below 1 metre during the evening.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southerly in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday, then decreasing to around 1 metre during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots turning southeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 2 to 3 metres through the Backstairs Passage.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
South to southeasterly about 10 knots.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres through the Backstairs Passage.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 25 knots turning southerly in the early afternoon. Winds reaching up to 30 knots in the late morning and again in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, tending southerly 4 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 25 knots decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the middle of the day.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday.
Southerly 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres around midday, then tending south to southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Below 0.5 metres, tending southerly 1 to 2 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
South to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to below 10 knots during the day.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Southerly 1 to 2 metres, decreasing below 0.5 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Salt Creek in the late morning and early afternoon. Winds turning southerly 15 to 25 knots in the late afternoon.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres by early evening.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm south of Salt Creek from the late morning.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots offshore early in the morning. Winds turning southeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the late afternoon.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday.
South to southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres by early evening, then tending southwesterly 2.5 metres later in the evening.
Below 0.5 metres, tending southerly 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
South to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots during the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Southerly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 0.5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
